health.mental_health: Heightened anxiety and hypervigilance related to ongoing workplace harassment; sleep disruption and occasional panic symptoms. Actively engaged in recovery supports and trying to maintain regular meals despite stress.

schedule.daily_routine: Early start with packed lunch and PPE check; travel to job sites across London; tries to take structured meal breaks to support recovery. Evenings usually include a short walk or cycle, journaling/documenting incidents, and preparing notes for meetings related to the harassment case.

Residential rewires for a refurbishment in Hackney (consumer unit upgrades, fault finding)

professional.performance_issues: Concentration has been impacted by stress; has requested to avoid working alone with a specific supervisor after repeated harassment. Taking extra time to double-check work and paperwork to prevent mistakes; concerned about being unfairly scrutinized or set up to fail.

Live music at small venues (when energy allows)
Reading crime fiction and non-fiction about workers’ rights
habits: Keeps a timestamped log of workplace incidents and saves relevant emails/messages
Meal planning with reminders to avoid skipping meals (recovery support)
Limits alcohol and avoids drinking when stressed to protect recovery
Uses mindfulness breathing exercises before sleep and before difficult meetings
Wears gloves and barrier cream regularly to manage hand dermatitis
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom flat with partner Jamie in East London; one room doubles as a small home office for paperwork, evidence organization, and union/legal calls. Prefers not to host work colleagues at home due to privacy concerns.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: No strict dietary restrictions; focuses on regular, balanced meals to support eating disorder recovery. Uses higher-protein snacks for long site days and avoids triggering diet talk; moderate caffeine to reduce anxiety.
